subject: [PATCH v2] fs: push nr_cached_objects memcg gating into individual filesystems

2026-07-16.patch_summary: This patch moves the check for whether a filesystem's nr_cached_objects hook is memcg-aware from fs/super.c to individual filesystems, allowing each filesystem to handle its own memcg awareness independently. The patch introduces a helper function mem_cgroup_shrink_is_root() in <linux/memcontrol.h> to simplify this check. This change fixes an issue where the assumption that non-root memcgs are not aware of nr_cached_objects was incorrect for XFS, which intentionally drives inode reclaim from per-memcg contexts. The patch also removes a gate in fs/super.c and adds support for XFS's inode-reclaim hook to be driven from per-memcg contexts.
